AdenHQ/Hive Judge System (Feb 23 2026). Layer 1: EventLoopNode implicit judge — post-execution per LLM turn, verdicts ACCEPT/RETRY/ESCALATE. Default rule-based: ACCEPT if no tool calls + output_keys present. LLMJudge: LiteLLM provider-agnostic. Level 2 (v0.4.4): evaluates full conversation vs success_criteria. judge_every_n_turns=1, max_iterations=50, max_retries=3. RETRY injects feedback as system msg. Layer 2: HybridJudge (ODD/goal-level) — verdicts ACCEPT/RETRY/REPLAN/ESCALATE. REPLAN at <90% weighted score. SuccessCriterion: id,description,metric(output_contains/equals/llm_judge/custom),target,weight. Constraints: hard→ESCALATE, soft→REPLAN+warn; categories: time/cost/safety/scope/quality. Evolution cycle: execute→evaluate→diagnose→regenerate (coding agent rewrites graph/prompts/edges). Key files: event_loop_node.py, node.py (NodeSpec), executor.py (GraphExecutor).

Context rot: GSD prevents via fresh start, Torus manages via selective state injection. State carryover: GSD explicit .planning/ files; Torus semantic memory + LIVE_STATE + SubagentStart. Pattern to adopt from GSD: reduce subagent injection to 50% target, explicit planning dir handoff, wave-based parallelism.

Passive Staking: TRv1 vs eclipse-agave. TRv1: STANDALONE program (Pass1veStake1111...). Instructions: InitializePassiveStake, ClaimRewards, Unlock, EarlyUnlock, CalculateEpochRewards (crank per account per epoch). State: PassiveStakeAccount 84 bytes (authority,amount,lock_days,lock_start,lock_end,unclaimed_rewards,last_reward_epoch,is_permanent,vote_weight_bps). Tiers in days: 0/30/90/180/360/permanent. Reward rates BPS: 500/1000/2000/3000/5000/12000. Early unlock: 5x tier rate (permanent cannot). ECLIPSE-AGAVE: INTEGRATED into stake program — PassiveLock(tier) and PermanentLock as StakeInstruction variants. Uses standard StakeStateV2. Passive = Stake with voter_pubkey=Pubkey::default() sentinel. Tiers 1-5 only. Lockup in StakeFlags bits 4-6 + lockup.epoch (14/36/72/144 epochs). Tier reward BPS: 25/50/100/150/250. PermanentLock: 12000 BPS (6% APY), PERMANENTLY_LOCKED flag. Rewards automatic via runtime epoch calculation. Early unlock: 5*rate_bp*stake/10000 to treasury. GovernanceUnlock stubbed. KEY DIFFERENCES: standalone vs integrated, manual crank vs automatic, tier 0 allowed vs required 1-5, inline lamports vs epoch system.

Passive Staking Reward Mechanisms: TRv1 vs eclipse-agave. FORMULAS: TRv1: reward_per_epoch=(amount*validator_rate_bp*tier_rate_bps)/(10000*10000*365), assumes 365 epochs/year. Eclipse-agave: passive_epoch_reward=stake*rate_bp/(10000*146), assumes 146 epochs/year (2.5-day epochs). DIFFERENT EPOCH ASSUMPTIONS = DIFFERENT AMOUNTS. TIER RATES (BPS of validator rate): TRv1 tier0=500/tier1=1000/tier2=2000/tier3=3000/tier4=5000/permanent=12000. Eclipse tier1=25/tier2=50/tier3=100/tier4=150/tier5=250 (of 5% base APY). Actual eclipse APY (5% validator): tier1~0.09%/tier2~0.17%/tier3~0.34%/tier4~0.51%/tier5~0.86%/permanent~6%. EARLY UNLOCK PENALTY: both use 5x tier_rate * stake/10000 sent to treasury; permanent cannot early-unlock. EPOCH TRACKING: TRv1 last_reward_epoch per account (crank invokes CalculateEpochRewards); eclipse via runtime StakeHistory + passive_epoch_reward in calculate_stake_vote_rewards per epoch. REWARD STORAGE: TRv1 inline lamports in PassiveStakeAccount; eclipse standard epoch rewards distribution system.

eclipse-agave CPI patterns for Phase 5 (SDK 2.2.0). BUILTIN ENTRYPOINT: declare_process_instruction!(Entrypoint, DEFAULT_COMPUTE_UNITS, |invoke_context|{...}) — same across stake, system, treasury, governance. NATIVE INVOKE (builtin-to-builtin CPI): invoke_context.native_invoke(instruction.into(), &[signers]) -> Result<(),InstructionError>; internally prepare_instruction()+process_instruction(). TX CONTEXT ACCESS: invoke_context.transaction_context.get_current_instruction_context()?, check_number_of_instruction_accounts(n)?, try_borrow_instruction_account(tx_ctx,idx)? for read/write; get_key_of_account_at_index(idx)? for pubkey without borrow. ACCOUNT BORROWING LIFETIME: must drop (end scope {}) before reborrowing at different index. SIGNERS: pass Pubkeys explicitly in signers slice for builtin-to-builtin CPI; PDA signers derived in-code. BANK vs NATIVE: bank-level code (fee_distribution.rs) can call get_account() directly; native programs must use native_invoke(). OPTIONAL ACCOUNTS: if get_number_of_instruction_accounts()>=6 { Some(5) } else { None }. ACCOUNT OWNERSHIP CHECK: account.get_owner()==&expected_program_id before reading data.

Safari iPad Picture-in-Picture limitations (Mar 2026). (1) canvas.captureStream() NOT supported in Safari iOS/iPad (WebKit Bug #181663) — blocks canvas→video→PiP entirely. (2) Safari PiP API is webkit-specific: webkitSupportsPresentationMode + webkitSetPresentationMode('picture-in-picture'|'inline'); standard requestPictureInPicture() not available; supported iPad Safari 9+, iPhone iOS 14+; NOT in Web App standalone mode. (3) DocumentPictureInPicture API NOT supported in any Safari (Chrome/Edge v116+ only, cannot do arbitrary HTML overlay). (4) Media Session API: basic metadata support, limited PiP integration. (5) PiP restricted to standard HTML5 <video> elements, no arbitrary HTML, floating controls limited. BOTTOM LINE: canvas-based overlays with dynamic content NOT feasible in Safari — only static HTML5 video can be placed in PiP.
